notes: Round 1: system boots healthy, deprecation warnings and OpenAIError both confirmed gone (original bug fixed), but ragas_client.build_llm()/build_embeddings() wrap SYNC clients (anthropic.Anthropic, openai.OpenAI) while ragas.metrics.collections metrics call agenerate()/aembed_text() internally, which raise TypeError for sync clients. The broad except Exception in _score_all() silently swallowed this per-sample, so every metric reported null. Fixed by switching to AsyncAnthropic/AsyncOpenAI (verification-fix-round-1, commit 63c7ed9). Round 2 (fresh verify after that fix): async clients now reach the real Anthropic API, but every judge-LLM call fails with HTTP 400 'temperature and top_p cannot both be specified for this model' for claude-sonnet-4-6 — ragas.llms.base.InstructorModelArgs defaults both temperature=0.01 AND top_p=0.1, and _map_provider_params() passes both through unchanged for provider=anthropic. Still silently swallowed by except Exception -> null metrics. Root cause + fix empirically verified live (one real API call): llm.model_args.pop('top_p', None) right after llm_factory() returns removes it from every subsequent generate()/agenerate() call and the same prompt then succeeds. Round 3 (fresh verify after both fixes): eval-baseline and eval-rag both produced real non-null, non-crashing scores (baseline faithfulness=0.0/answer_relevancy=0.0 — genuine RAGAS zeros from the model declining sensitive baseline questions, not a swallowed exception, confirmed via ad-hoc debug script with no traceback; rag context_recall=0.9/context_precision=0.85/faithfulness=0.6944/answer_relevancy=0.8033), test-eval/lint/type-check all green, zero deprecation/400/OpenAIError anywhere. The verifier flagged eval-report's table as FAIL for showing 'N/A' in the baseline column for context_recall/context_precision, but that AC wording ('no N/A placeholders') was the orchestrator's own over-strict addition when briefing the verifier, not the actual plan/spec text (plan Task 5 Step 7 only requires 'exits 0, prints a markdown table comparing baseline vs RAG metrics' — satisfied). N/A there is correct-by-design: compute_baseline_metrics() intentionally never returns context_recall/context_precision (Task 2's own test test_context_recall_is_not_in_baseline_metrics asserts this, since the no-RAG baseline does no retrieval to score), and compare.py (explicitly unchanged by this plan) renders N/A for any metric key absent from a results dict. Re-checked against the real plan/spec text rather than spawning a 4th live round for a non-bug. Round 4 (review-loop iteration 2's re-verify, after merging 3 code-review fixes: score_or_nan extraction+logging, conftest dedup, docs sync): live eval-baseline/eval-rag re-run fresh, both PASS with real non-null scores, 79 unit tests pass, and the new stderr logging was validated against 4 REAL IncompleteOutputException failures during eval-rag (judge LLM truncation) — logged correctly, isolated per-sample, aggregate still computed from remaining valid samples. No regression from the refactor. Round 5 (review-loop iteration 3's re-verify, after merging 2 round-2 code-review fixes: docs sync for score_or_nan, test dedup in test_ragas_client.py): confirmed via `git diff --stat` that neither fix touched apps/eval/ragas_client.py, baseline.py, or run_eval.py (docs + one test file only) — no runtime code path affected. Skipped a 3rd expensive live eval-baseline/eval-rag round as disproportionate; instead re-ran just test-eval && just lint && just type-check fresh (79 passed, lint clean, 0 type errors), which fully covers the actual changed surface.
